route.name: Aspen Ridge Overnighter
route.region: Buena Vista to Salida / Aspen Ridge
route.description: Public GPX point-to-point overnighter on dirt roads, gravel, primitive camping corridors, and paved town access.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX point-to-point overnighter on dirt roads, gravel, primitive camping corridors, and paved town access.
route.bestSeason: Spring through fall; July requires heat and afternoon thunderstorm checks.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX point-to-point overnighter on dirt roads, gravel, primitive camping corridors, and paved town access.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Trout Creek is a key reliable source; confirm current flow and carry enough between sources.
resources.food: Full services and bike shops in Buena Vista and Salida.
resources.camping: Primitive camps are common after about mile 29; Browns Canyon restrictions and weekend pressure need verification.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Clean Sat-Sun shape if the shuttle/logistics are solved.
Lock the camp zone before promoting.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Not gate-confirmed until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
No exact legal overnight plan for the route dates.
Primitive camp waypoints are not enough proof where Browns Canyon/designated-site limitations may apply.
Stage 2 fire restrictions affect the Salida Ranger District; stove/camp protocol must be written into the plan.
No final weather, smoke/AQI, Red Flag, or closure refresh close enough to departure.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh before next-weekend gate confirmation.
Call/verify Salida Ranger District or BLM Browns Canyon for the exact camp zone and whether the GPX camp is legal.
Confirm lodging or legal campsite for July 11, 2026, with backup.
Overlay the GPX against PSICC/BLM current orders and Browns Canyon boundaries.
Measure water gaps and set a conservative carry after Trout Creek.
Refresh NWS forecast, Red Flag/Fire Weather Watch, AirNow/smoke, PSICC alerts, and BLM alerts July 8-10 and ride mornings.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Clean Sat-Sun shape, conditional on heat, thunderstorms, camp legality, and shuttle logistics.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
